titleStmt.title: Letter from David Wilson to WB Yeats, 11 April 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: David Wilson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

msIdentifier.repository: Harry Ransom Center, The University of Texas at Austin
msIdentifier.collection: William Butler Yeats Collection; II. Correspondence, 1891-1938, Outgoing. Box 8. Folder: Unidentified - Z (8.3)

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
66 Kenilworth Rd
St Leonards-on-Sea 11th April 1917 Dear Mr Yeats: I think I told you in my last
that today was the one on which I should probably have trouble
with the military authorities when therefore the cards turned
up I was compelled to test only one pack in a very hurried
and unsatisfactory manner. What I call the position
test gives the four of spades - the negative test gives
the King of Diamonds. Presumably therefore this card is
the four of spades- Owing to the letter being an invitation in which I
had to carry out this test I expect it is all wrong. Please
write and tell me if this is so but do not say what the
card was. In the meanwhile perforce I will have to delay
the test of the other pack, although if I am hindered
long the aura will have vanished. Sincerely yours
David Wilson
